title: Construct String from Binary Tree
titleCn: 根据二叉树创建字符串

testcases.input: root = [1,2,3,4] Output: "1(2(4))(3)" Explanation: Originally, it needs to be "1(2(4)())(3()())", but you need to omit all the empty parenthesis pairs. And it will be "1(2(4))(3)".
testcases.expectedOutput: "1(2(4))(3)" Explanation: Originally, it needs to be "1(2(4)())(3()())", but you need to omit all the empty parenthesis pairs. And it will be "1(2(4))(3)".

testcases.input: root = [1,2,3,null,4] Output: "1(2()(4))(3)" Explanation: Almost the same as the first example, except the () after 2 is necessary to indicate the absence of a left child for 2 and the presence of a right child.
testcases.expectedOutput: "1(2()(4))(3)" Explanation: Almost the same as the first example, except the () after 2 is necessary to indicate the absence of a left child for 2 and the presence of a right child.
